nc_heading: Non-Compliance: Not Designated for Pest Control
nc_text: The pest control locker should be dedicated to pest control items only and have restricted access.
nc_rem_text: The Pest Control Responsible Officer (PCRO) and Pest Control Officer (Pest Control Officer) should immediately address this non-compliance.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.

nc_text: A pest control locker cannot be located in a food area.
nc_rem_text: The Pest Control Responsible Officer (PCRO) and Pest Control Officer (Pest Control Officer) should immediately address this non-compliance.

nc_text: There are insufficient fly killers on this ship. A separate report with more details will be included with this audit report.
nc_rem_text: The Pest Control Responsible Officer (PCRO) and Pest Control Officer (Pest Control Officer) should immediately address this non-compliance.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.

nc_text: There are zapper type [killer grid] fly killers on this ship and they should eb removed. They should never be used in food areas, and they are poor at controlling the small fruit flies as the killer grid is mainly ineffective and why glue board type fly killers are recommended.
nc_rem_text: The Pest Control Responsible Officer (PCRO) and Pest Control Officer (Pest Control Officer) should immediately address this non-compliance.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.

nc_text: Rat guards have been installed that do not meet the minimum height or width construction requirements, which are: • 200mm high from the top of the mooring line. • 200 mm wide on either side of the mooring line
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: There is no one rat guard per line
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: All rat guards should be installed lesss than one hour after arrival
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: All rat guards must be left in place and not removed more than one hour prior to departure
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: All gaps should be filled with non-chewable material.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: Subject to safety considerations, all rat guards should be a minimum of 2 metres from the dockside.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: All rat guards should be at 90 degrees and not too sloped.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: Rat guards should be tied in place and not allowed to fall and be hanging.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: Cone-shaped rat guards have an orientation where the cone should be facing the ship, which has not happened on this occasion.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: Rats can jump up to 1200mm (48 inches) and is important that they are not tied too close to the ground.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: The securing or safety line tying the rat guard to the mooring or spring lines should ensure that these are always tied behind the rat guard, so that the rodents cannot use them to climb over the rat guard.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: The rat guards are not weighted as per the IPM Plan. The weights ensure that the rat guards are not blown off in the wind and stay secure.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: Rat guards should be a minimum of 60 cms away from the ship to prevent them jumping on board.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_heading: Non-Compliance: Too Close to Access Holes
nc_text: Rat guards should be tied before any rope access holes to prevent them jumping through these gaps.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.
nc_ipm_ref: This would be the IPMPlan Reference and other information

nc_text: The cockroach traps were not folded and assembled correctly.
nc_rem_text: The Pest Control Responsible Officer (PCRO), Pest Control Officer (Pest Control Officer) and Department Head should immediately address this non-compliance.
nc_usph_text: This woud be the USPH/VSP Reference and text.

nc_text: The ramps on the cockroach traps must be folded in.
nc_rem_text: The Pest Control Responsible Officer (PCRO), Pest Control Officer (Pest Control Officer) and Department Head should immediately address this non-compliance.
